title: jacea pseudophrygia
intro: Jacea pseudophrygia, also known as Yarrow or Achillea millefolium, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is an herb that has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Jacea pseudophrygia include reducing pain and inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, as well as being used to treat menstrual cramps and other women's health issues. Herbal preparations of Jacea pseudophrygia include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent, and can be used to treat a variety of ailments.
